input.name: Sarah Chen
input.title: VP Engineering
input.message: We're evaluating MDK for our internal agent platform. Budget approved for Q2 ($150k initial, $400k expansion potential). Want to see a live demo and detailed pricing this week. Already eliminated 2 alternatives in our shortlist; need to make a decision by end of month.

budget.rationale: Budget explicitly approved for Q2 with concrete figures: $150k initial + $400k expansion path.

authority.rationale: VP-level title, technical decision-maker for an internal agent platform initiative.

need.rationale: Active evaluation, already shortlisted, building an internal agent platform — direct fit.

timeline.rationale: Wants demo this week, decision by end of month.

expected.rationale: Textbook qualified — budget approved, authority confirmed, active need, urgent timeline. Schedule a discovery + demo combo call this week with the AE and an SE. Bring pricing for both the $150k tier and the $400k expansion path.
objections: pricing comparison vs in-house build
shortlist competitors — needs differentiated SE story

input.name: Marcus Rodriguez
input.title: Director of Engineering Platforms
input.message: Met your team at the AI Summit. Interested in scaling our internal LLM tooling. We have ~50 internal use cases in early POCs, no central platform yet. CFO has flagged AI tooling as a 2026 investment area; specific budget not yet allocated. Looking at Q3-Q4 for vendor selection.

budget.rationale: AI tooling flagged as 2026 investment area by CFO, but no specific budget allocated yet. Likely to materialize but not concrete.

authority.rationale: Director of Engineering Platforms — directly responsible for the use case, will be the technical buyer.

need.rationale: 50 internal LLM POCs without a central platform — strong consolidation need.

timeline.rationale: Q3-Q4 vendor selection. Real but not urgent.

expected.rationale: Strong qualified opportunity, just not as urgent as a hot deal. Schedule a 30-min intro call within 2 weeks. Goal: understand the 50 use cases, validate the budget timeline with CFO directly, and become the reference architecture they design around. Send the consolidation case studies before the call.
objections: budget not yet allocated — risk of slipping to 2027
internal build pressure given Globex's eng headcount

input.name: Priya Patel
input.title: Senior Engineer
input.message: Signed up for the free trial yesterday. Spent 2 hours kicking the tires. Looks promising — particularly interested in the eval features. Curious about how teams structure their agent libraries; do you have customer case studies I could read?

budget.rationale: Free trial, no budget signal. Engineer-level user, likely needs to escalate any purchase decision.

authority.rationale: Senior engineer, not a buyer. May be a champion but won't sign a contract.

need.rationale: Active trial usage, interested in eval features — specific use case forming.

timeline.rationale: No timeline mentioned. Trial exploration mode.

expected.rationale: Classic champion-track lead — engineer using the product, but not the buyer. Don't push for a meeting yet; build the champion. Send the requested case studies, follow up in 2 weeks to ask about her trial findings, and ask 'who else at TechFlow would be interested in seeing this?' to surface the buyer. Tag her account in CRM as champion-track.
objections: non-buyer — needs internal champion building
may not have internal budget visibility

input.name: Joe
input.title: Hobbyist
input.message: Cool tool! Just downloaded the whitepaper. Is this free for personal projects?

authority.rationale: Sole operator but no enterprise context.

need.rationale: Interested but no specific use case shared.

expected.rationale: Low-intent content download from a hobbyist. Add to free-tier nurture drip rather than burning SDR cycles. Auto-reply pointing him at the free-tier docs + community forum.
objections: pricing — likely won't convert to paid

input.name: Dr. Lisa Wang
input.title: Research Faculty
input.message: I'm planning a research project on agent evaluation methodologies and would like to use MDK for the experimental framework. Looking for an academic license. Project starts in September, runs through next May.

budget.rationale: Academic project — typically minimal budget. We have an academic free-tier program but it's not a commercial sale.

authority.rationale: PI on the research project; full authority over tooling choices but not a commercial buyer.

need.rationale: Specific research project using MDK as experimental framework — strong product fit.

timeline.rationale: Project starts in September with a defined endpoint.

expected.rationale: Not a commercial opportunity, but high strategic value. Route to the academic partnerships team — they have a free-tier program for research with the trade-off of citation in publications + a case study. Could generate referenceable academic adoption. Don't burn SDR cycles selling.
objections: non-commercial — strategic-only value

input.name: Chen Wei
input.company: Beijing AI Solutions (50 employees, AI consulting)
input.title: CTO
input.message: Got your email. We help SMB clients in China integrate AI tools. Would MDK be available in China? Pricing for resellers?

budget.rationale: Reseller channel, would route end-customer budget through them. Budget exists in principle.

need.rationale: Reseller use case is real but introduces channel complexity (pricing tiers, support tiers, regional compliance).

timeline.rationale: Cold outbound response, no urgency expressed.

expected.rationale: Reseller inquiry from a region with significant operational complexity (China data residency, payment rails, US export controls on AI services). Not a standard SDR lead. Route to the partnerships team for qualification — they handle the reseller program separately and have legal/compliance pre-checks. Reply with the partnership program intake form.
objections: China-specific compliance & data residency
reseller pricing not yet defined for emerging markets

input.name: Tom Bradley
input.title: VP IT Security
input.message: We're issuing an RFP for AI development tooling. SOC 2 Type II is mandatory; HIPAA BAA + IL-4 federal compliance preferred. Looking for an enterprise platform with strong audit logging. RFP deadline 6 weeks out. Pricing flexibility expected for a 3-year commitment.

budget.rationale: RFP with explicit 3-year commitment language — significant budget allocated.

authority.rationale: VP IT Security — strong influence on the compliance/security side, but the technical buyer (likely VP Engineering or CDO) wasn't mentioned. Need to find them.

need.rationale: Active RFP with specific compliance requirements — direct platform fit if we meet the bar.

expected.rationale: Enterprise RFP with active timeline, real budget, and strong compliance fit (we have SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA BAA available). Engage the AE + compliance lead immediately. Goal of first call: identify the technical buyer (not in the message), understand the eval criteria weighting, and confirm whether IL-4 is a hard requirement (we don't have IL-4 yet — that could disqualify us). Pull the SOC 2 report + HIPAA BAA template into a procurement-ready packet.
objections: IL-4 federal compliance not yet held — may need a roadmap commitment or a partnered FedRAMP shop
technical buyer not identified yet — need a meeting to map the buying committee
3-year commitment requires CFO + legal sign-off on our side

input.name: Emma Schultz
input.title: Founder/CEO
input.message: Hi! Joined your webinar last week. We're a 6-month-old YC startup building consumer AI. No funding yet beyond YC's $500k. Loved the platform overview. Would love to chat about how to use MDK on a tight budget.

budget.rationale: Limited budget ($500k YC funding for a 8-person company across 18-month runway). Could afford the Pro tier but not Enterprise.

need.rationale: Building consumer AI — product fit exists but vague specifics.

timeline.rationale: No explicit timeline; startup exploration mode.

expected.rationale: Founder-CEO at a YC startup is high-quality fit for our startup program: 12-month free Pro tier in exchange for case study + reference participation. The startup program account team handles these, not SDR. Refer her, send her the startup program landing page link, and add her to the founders' Slack community for ongoing engagement.
objections: limited budget for next 12 months
may churn at end of free-tier program if traction doesn't materialize

input.name: Alex Kowalski
input.title: Senior Engineer III
input.message: Hi, I'm trying to figure out how the eval-scorecard CLI compares to OpenAI's evals framework. Does MDK support running evals against multiple models at once?

budget.rationale: Banking enterprise — significant budget capacity in principle, but Senior Engineer III is several layers below the buying committee.

authority.rationale: Senior Engineer III — not a buyer. May be evaluating personally or for an internal team.

need.rationale: Specific technical question about a real product feature (multi-model eval). Suggests evaluation activity, possibly POC-phase.

expected.rationale: Two-step: (1) FIRST answer his technical question — multi-model eval support is a strength (cross-family judge enforcement, --judge-model flag). Use the answer to build rapport. (2) Then ask 'is this for a team initiative, or personal exploration?' If team initiative at a Major Bank, escalate to the enterprise AE team. If personal, route to nurture. Don't sell heavy — engineers smell it.
objections: non-buyer — need to identify the buyer first

input.name: Spam Bot 47
input.title: Marketing Director
input.message: Hi! I would like to receive more information about your services to potentially explore opportunities. Please send pricing brochures and case studies via email. Thank you and best regards.

budget.rationale: No budget signal whatsoever. Generic AI-generated outreach pattern.

authority.rationale: Title implausible for company described; signals form-spam.

need.rationale: No specific use case mentioned. Boilerplate text.

timeline.rationale: No timeline. No urgency. No specifics.

expected.rationale: Classic form-spam pattern: generic boilerplate, AI-generated wording, temp-email domain, no specifics. Mark as disqualified in CRM. Don't add to nurture (degrades the nurture pool's quality). If volume from this domain pattern increases, flag to ops for form-bot defenses.
objections: not a real lead

input.name: Jennifer Park
input.title: Director of Engineering
input.message: Mike Liu at FastSaaS gave me your contact — he's been using MDK for 6 months and raved about your eval framework. We have a similar problem: 12 internal AI tools, no centralized evaluation. Looking to fix this in the next quarter. Mike said you guys are easy to work with on procurement.

budget.rationale: Referral with implicit budget signal — Mike at FastSaaS is on a paid tier, and Jennifer's company would likely match. Not explicitly confirmed.

authority.rationale: Director of Engineering with a clear platform mandate, named the problem (12 internal AI tools, no central eval).

need.rationale: Specific use case mirrors the referrer's success story. Strong product fit.

timeline.rationale: Next quarter — defined window, ~12 weeks out.

expected.rationale: High-quality referral with named champion (Mike at FastSaaS). Book a 45-min discovery within 1 week. Bring Mike's case study + a tailored ROI calculator for 12 internal tools. Ask Jennifer to invite the team that owns those 12 tools to a follow-up demo. Treat as a fast-moving warm lead.
objections: competing internal-build temptation (mid-market eng teams sometimes prefer to build)
needs to validate Mike's experience matches her stack

input.name: Dmitri Ivanov
input.title: Product Manager
input.message: Hi! Saw your post about agent evaluation best practices. We're in stealth, ~5 people, building a vertical AI agent for the legal industry. No funding closed yet (Seed round in discussions). Curious to learn from your team's approach to multi-judge eval frameworks.

authority.rationale: Founding PM at a small team — full authority once funded, but not yet.

need.rationale: Building an AI agent product, eval framework relevant. Specific use case (legal vertical) but stealth mode.

expected.rationale: Pre-funding founder networking, not a sales opportunity yet. Reply personally with a useful response to his question (build the relationship), invite him to the founders' Slack, and add him to the 'pre-funded founders' nurture list. Re-qualify in 6 months once they've closed Seed. Common pattern: these become $20k-$50k Pro-tier deals 9-12 months later if you keep the relationship warm.
objections: pre-funding — no purchase capacity for 6-12 months
